input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-04-08T00:59:49.250Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` to provide Light/Dark values inline:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework reads the device's current `AppTheme` and picks the matching value. When the user toggles system dark mode, every `AppThemeBinding` re-evaluates automatically — no event wiring needed.

---

**C# alternative** — use `SetAppTheme` or `SetAppThemeColor` in code-behind:

```csharp
// For Color properties specifically:
myLabel.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
    light: Colors.Black,
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

// For any property (generic version):
myPage.SetAppTheme<Color>(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    light: Colors.White,
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));

// You can also detect the current theme manually:
AppTheme current = Application.Current.RequestedTheme;
// returns AppTheme.Light, AppTheme.Dark, or AppTheme.Unspecified
```

---

**Key points:**

| Concern | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Automatic updates** | Both approaches react to OS theme changes at runtime |
| **Resource dictionaries** | For reuse, define `AppThemeBinding` values in `App.xaml` styles instead of inline |
| **Manual override** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark` to force a theme regardless of system setting |

For a larger app, move colors into a `<Style>` in your `ResourceDictionary` so you define each `AppThemeBinding` once rather than on every element.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for a tutorial/explanation on how to use `AppThemeBinding` in .NET MAUI to support light/dark theme switching. This is a knowledge question — they want a XAML example and a C# explanation. No codebase to modify, just guidance.
